output.summary: Replace JS-driven sticky behavior in Tabs block with native CSS position:sticky by adding an intermediate wrapper div inside <details> to work around the browser limitation where position:sticky fails on direct children of <details> elements.
output.analysis:
## Root Cause Analysis CSS `position: sticky` does not work on a **direct child** of `
` elements, even when the `
` has `display: flex` and `overflow: visible`. This is a browser-level limitation in how `
` establishes its internal layout context. The user confirmed this by testing: replacing `
` with `
` made CSS sticky work immediately. ## Current DOM Structure (desktop, details open) ```
...
...
``` The JS workaround (`updateStickyPanel` in `Tabs.js`) uses `transform: translateY()` on scroll via `requestAnimationFrame` to simulate sticky behavior. This is ~35 lines of JS that had a NaN bug with `--fixed-elements-height` CSS variable parsing and adds unnecessary complexity. ## Proposed Solution: Intermediate Wrapper Add a new `
` wrapper between `
` and `.tabs-item-inner`. This wrapper is a direct child of `
` (so it inherits the flex-1 stretching behavior), and `.tabs-item-inner` becomes a child of the wrapper (no longer a direct child of `
`). ### New DOM Structure (desktop, details open) ```
...
...
``` ### Why This Works 1. `.tabs-item-body` is the direct child of `
` -- it does NOT need to be sticky, it just needs to stretch to full height (flex:1) 2. `.tabs-item-inner` is now a child of `.tabs-item-body` (a normal `
`), not a direct child of `
` 3. Since `.tabs-item-body` has no overflow constraint and is taller than `.tabs-item-inner`, the sticky element can stick relative to the viewport scroll 4. The background color on `
` (`md:open:bg-accent-lighter`) and its padding (`md:open:p-6`) remain unchanged 5. Mobile behavior is unaffected -- the wrapper is transparent on mobile ### What Gets Removed - `updateStickyPanel()` function (lines 56-84) -- 28 lines of JS - `clearStickyTransforms()` function (lines 86-93) -- 7 lines - `onScroll()` function and event listener (lines 95-103) -- 8 lines - `stickyRafId` variable declaration - All `clearStickyTransforms()` calls in `selectTab()` and `setMobileMode()` - All `updateStickyPanel()` calls in `selectTab()` - The scroll event listener registration Total: ~45 lines of JS removed, replaced by ~3 CSS classes.

fileChanges.description: Add intermediate wrapper div and CSS sticky classes to .tabs-item-inner
fileChanges.details:
1. Wrap the existing `.tabs-item-inner` div with a new `
` wrapper. This div sits between `` and `
`, and closes after the `.tabs-item-inner` closing tag. 2. Add sticky classes to `.tabs-item-inner`: change from `
` to `
`. 3. Remove the JS comment about JS-driven sticky positioning. Resulting structure: ```php
...
```

fileChanges.description: Add matching intermediate wrapper div in editor component
fileChanges.details:
1. Add a `<div className="tabs-item-body md:flex-1 md:overflow-visible md:relative">` wrapper around the `.tabs-item-inner` div.

2. The `.tabs-item-inner` already has `md:sticky md:top-[calc(var(--fixed-elements-height)+32px)] md:self-start` classes (line 136), so those should be kept.

Note: The editor may render `<details>` differently than the frontend, but adding the wrapper ensures structural parity between editor and frontend.

fileChanges.description: Remove all JS-driven sticky positioning code
fileChanges.details:
Remove the following from Tabs.js:

1. Delete the `stickyRafId` variable declaration (line 28)
2. Delete the entire `updateStickyPanel()` function (lines 56-84)
3. Delete the entire `clearStickyTransforms()` function (lines 86-93)
4. Delete the entire `onScroll()` function (lines 95-101)
5. Delete the `window.addEventListener('scroll', onScroll, { passive: true })` line (line 103)
6. In `selectTab()`: remove the `inner.style.transform = ''` line inside the forEach loop (lines 134-136), and remove the `requestAnimationFrame(updateStickyPanel)` call at the end (lines 145-147)
7. In `setMobileMode()`: remove the `clearStickyTransforms()` call (line 242)
8. Update the top-of-file comment to remove mention of JS-driven sticky and instead note that CSS sticky is used with an intermediate wrapper

This reduces Tabs.js by approximately 45 lines. The remaining JS handles:
- ARIA setup
- Desktop tab selection (open/close details)
- Keyboard navigation
- Summary click prevention on desktop
- Responsive mode switching
- Initialization

designDecisions.question: How to make CSS position:sticky work inside <details> elements?
designDecisions.decision: Add an intermediate wrapper <div class='tabs-item-body'> between <details> and .tabs-item-inner. This avoids the browser limitation where sticky fails on direct children of <details>. The wrapper takes flex:1 to stretch full height, providing the tall containing block that sticky requires.
designDecisions.evidence: User confirmed that replacing <details> with <div> makes CSS sticky work, proving the issue is specific to <details> direct children. Adding an intermediate <div> wrapper moves .tabs-item-inner out of the direct-child relationship.
designDecisions.recommendation: Use the intermediate wrapper approach. It requires no browser-specific CSS, no JavaScript, and preserves the <details>/<summary> pattern for mobile accordion behavior.

designDecisions.question: Should the sticky offset account for --fixed-elements-height CSS variable?
designDecisions.decision: Yes, use md:top-[calc(var(--fixed-elements-height)+32px)] which matches the existing tablist sticky offset and the previous JS implementation's stickyTop calculation.
designDecisions.evidence: The tablist already uses md:top-[calc(var(--fixed-elements-height)+32px)] for its own sticky positioning (Tabs.php line 32). The JS implementation also used this exact formula (Tabs.js line 63-64).
designDecisions.recommendation: Keep the same offset for both tablist and panel content.

designDecisions.question: Should we add md:self-start to .tabs-item-inner to prevent it from stretching to full height?
designDecisions.decision: Not needed in PHP. The sticky element naturally sizes to its content. The TSX editor component already has md:self-start but this is for editor-specific rendering. The CSS sticky behavior itself constrains the element to content height.
designDecisions.evidence: A sticky element remains in the normal flow and sizes to its content. It only offsets its visual position when the scroll position would move it past the sticky offset.
designDecisions.recommendation: Do not add md:self-start to the PHP template. If testing reveals the sticky element stretches unexpectedly, add it as a follow-up fix.

designDecisions.question: What happens to the selectTab() function's transform clearing code?
designDecisions.decision: Remove all transform-related code from selectTab(). Since CSS sticky is declarative, switching tabs only requires opening/closing the details elements. The browser handles the sticky positioning automatically.
designDecisions.evidence: CSS position:sticky is declarative -- the browser continuously evaluates the sticky offset during scroll. No manual recalculation needed when tab content changes.
designDecisions.recommendation: Remove transform clearing and sticky update calls from selectTab().

designDecisions.question: Does the mobile accordion behavior need any changes?
designDecisions.decision: No. The new wrapper div is transparent on mobile (no special classes). The <details>/<summary> behavior is unchanged. On mobile, overflow-hidden on <details> and the normal accordion open/close pattern work as before.
designDecisions.evidence: The wrapper only gets md: prefixed classes (md:flex-1, md:overflow-visible, md:relative). On mobile (below md breakpoint), it renders as a plain div with no styling impact.

designDecisions.question: Should we verify that the sticky approach works by checking if <details> overflow-hidden on mobile doesn't leak into desktop?
designDecisions.decision: The existing classes already handle this: the <details> has overflow-hidden (base) and md:overflow-visible (desktop override). This is correct for sticky to work on desktop while keeping overflow-hidden for clean accordion animations on mobile.
designDecisions.evidence: TabsItem.php line 20-22: overflow-hidden is base, md:overflow-visible overrides on desktop. The wrapper div also gets md:overflow-visible explicitly.
designDecisions.recommendation: Keep existing overflow classes. No changes needed on <details> itself.

risks.risk: The intermediate wrapper approach may not solve the sticky issue if the browser limitation extends to all descendants of <details>, not just direct children
risks.mitigation: The user confirmed that a <div> replacement makes sticky work, strongly suggesting the issue is about the <details> element's own box model, not about descendant traversal. The wrapper approach creates a normal <div> box between <details> and the sticky element. If this fails, a CSS-only fallback using the ::details-content pseudo-element could be tried.

risks.risk: The wrapper div may interfere with the flex layout or change spacing/padding behavior
risks.mitigation: The wrapper has no padding or margin. It only gets flex-1 on desktop to stretch. Visual QA should verify that the background, padding, and spacing remain identical.

qaChecklist: Desktop: Verify sticky content stays pinned at top when scrolling long tab content (especially the 'All Services' 8-tab variation and 'Long Content' variation)
Desktop: Switch between tabs and verify the new tab's content becomes sticky immediately
Desktop: Verify the background color (bg-accent-lighter) still fills the full height of the panel
Desktop: Verify padding (p-6) on the details element is visually unchanged
Desktop: Verify colored tabs (neon-green, blue, orange, purple) still render correctly
Mobile: Verify accordion open/close behavior is unchanged
Mobile: Verify no sticky artifacts appear on mobile
Mobile: Verify the accordion expand/collapse animation is smooth
Resize: Switch from desktop to mobile and back -- verify mode transitions work
Keyboard: Verify ArrowUp/ArrowDown navigation and Enter/Space selection still work on desktop
Verify --fixed-elements-height offset works correctly with sticky header
